input.background: A group of 115,818 women between 30 and 55 years of age were recruited to be in a study. Individuals were free of coronary heart disease at the time of recruitment. Body weight of subjects was determined as of 1976. Let us call this variable WT 1976. Weight was also determined as of age 18. Let's call this variable WI_18. From these variables, the investigators calculated weight change for individuals (WT_CHNG = WT_1976 - WT_18). Adult height in meters was determined (H1) and was used to calculate body mass index according to the formula: BMI - weight in kilograms ÷ (height in meters)^2. BMI was determined as of age 18 (BMI_18) and at the time of recruitment in 1976 (BMI_1976). All of these variables are quantitative.
input.question: The study followed individuals over time and monitored whether they experienced adverse coronary events. A new variable (let us call it CORONARY) would then be used to record this new information. CORONARY is a categorical variable with two possible values: either the person did or did not experience an adverse coronary event. During the first 14 years of follow-up, there were 1292 such events.

WT_1976.description: Body weight of individuals measured at the time of recruitment in 1976.

WT_18.value: Body weight of subjects at age 18
WT_18.description: Body weight of individuals measured at age 18.

WT_CHNG.description: Weight change calculated as the difference between weight in 1976 and weight at age 18.

H1.description: Adult height of individuals measured in meters.

BMI_18.description: Body mass index of individuals calculated at age 18.

BMI_1976.description: Body mass index of individuals calculated at the time of recruitment in 1976.

value: Did not experience adverse coronary event
CORONARY.description: Whether individuals experienced an adverse coronary event during the follow-up period.

meta_info.caption: Weight change and coronary heart disease.
